# Vuk Dinic

> Vuk Dinic is a computational sociologist and data scientist. He is a PhD
> researcher in the Datavysts Marie Skłodowska-Curie doctoral network, hosted at
> the Jožef Stefan Institute in Ljubljana, Slovenia, where his work focuses on
> building trustworthy AI tools to increase political participation. He writes
> about politics, language, and power, explored through data science, natural
> language processing, and machine learning.

This site is part blog, part lab notebook. Content is authored by Vuk Dinic and
licensed under the GNU GPL v3.

## Key pages

- [About](https://vukdinic.com/about/): Who Vuk Dinic is, his research interests, and how to reach him.
- [Now](https://vukdinic.com/now/): What Vuk is working on, reading, and thinking about at the moment.
- [Projects](https://vukdinic.com/projects/): Research and data-science work, including an NLP analysis of the Serbian Parliament.
- [Posts](https://vukdinic.com/posts/): Essays on data, society, and politics.
- [Notes](https://vukdinic.com/notes/): Shorter, rougher thoughts and reading notes.

## Topics

- Computational social science and political discourse analysis
- Natural language processing for low-resource languages (e.g. South Slavic languages)
- Trustworthy and explainable AI and its role in democratic participation
- Human–computer interaction

- GitHub: https://github.com/realvuk
